thesis_summary: Highest scanner conviction (7.0) with a clear cloud capex tailwind confirmed by Google's earnings beat. Diversified cloud ETF reduces single-name risk while capturing the secular hyperscaler spend cycle.
catalyst: Google earnings beat signaling accelerating cloud capex boom flowing into free cash flow across cloud infrastructure names.
technical_setup: RSI 39.6 — not overbought, leaving room to run; constructive mean-reversion entry rather than chasing extended momentum.
risk_factors: Cloud capex could compress near-term FCF; broad tech multiple compression if rates back up. 8% stop / 20% TP enforced.

thesis_summary: Record revenue with strong AI networking/custom silicon franchise and balanced RSI offers entry without chasing. Buffett-style: durable moat, strong cash generation, dominant in AI connectivity.
catalyst: Q4 2025 earnings call highlighting record revenue and AI-driven backlog growth.
technical_setup: RSI 50.7 — neutral and healthiest among semis, no overbought condition, room for continuation.
risk_factors: Rich valuation; AI capex digestion risk. Stop loss at 8%.

thesis_summary: Provides non-semiconductor diversification (space/aerospace innovation) while still RISK-ON aligned. Outperforming JETS with higher returns and an oversold-neutral RSI gives an attractive non-correlated tilt.
catalyst: Relative outperformance vs. JETS and broad innovation/aerospace momentum.
technical_setup: RSI 46.9 — neutral, not extended, supports a measured entry.
risk_factors: Higher-beta thematic ETF, less earnings-anchored; vulnerable in any volatility spike. Tight stop discipline applies.

thesis_summary: Foundry monopoly with pristine balance sheet and the most direct, durable beneficiary of AI silicon demand. Earnings beat confirms the fundamental thesis with reasonable valuation versus peers.
catalyst: TSMC earnings beat reinforcing strong AI-driven foundry demand.
technical_setup: RSI 60.1 — moderately strong but not overbought, MA alignment positive in uptrend.
risk_factors: Geopolitical Taiwan risk; AI bubble narrative could pressure sentiment. 8% stop enforced.

thesis_summary: Alphabet offers a pristine balance sheet, dominant search/cloud franchise, and AI monetization optionality (Gemini) at a reasonable valuation. Adds mega-cap software/internet exposure that meaningfully diversifies away from the portfolio's current semiconductor concentration (AVGO/TSM/CLOU).
catalyst: Strongest 1Q2026 earnings momentum cited by scanner; AI/Gemini monetization and cloud growth re-rating.
technical_setup: RSI neutral at 49.1 — not overbought, leaving room to run; no momentum chase. Constructive base-building setup ahead of catalyst.
risk_factors: Regulatory/antitrust overhang, AI capex spend pressuring margins, broad mega-cap tech beta.

thesis_summary: Microsoft pairs Buffett-grade balance sheet quality with the strongest enterprise AI distribution via Azure and the renewed OpenAI partnership. The deeply oversold RSI (34.9) on a STRONG BUY franchise offers an attractive mean-reversion entry into a high-quality non-semi name.
catalyst: Next chapter of Microsoft-OpenAI partnership; Azure AI revenue acceleration.
technical_setup: RSI 34.9 — oversold on a structurally uptrending name, favorable risk/reward bounce setup. Catalyst + chart both confirm a non-momentum-chase entry.
risk_factors: OpenAI restructuring uncertainty, AI capex intensity, premium valuation if growth disappoints.

exit_reason: (1) Macro is neutral but ARKX offers no specific catalyst tailwind in the current AI/semiconductor-led tape. (2) The space/aerospace diversification thesis has not generated alpha — position is down -5.2% with no company-level catalyst driving the underlying basket. (3) Technically ARKX is in a weak downtrend below key moving averages with no reversal pattern; the scanner shows no signal supporting it versus ARKQ at only 6.0. (4) Exiting frees a slot and ~$15,166 cash to rotate into higher-conviction semiconductor signals scoring 8.0+. (5) Primary risk is exiting before a space-sector rebound, but the opportunity cost of holding a thesis-less, underperforming ETF in a strong semi tape outweighs that — capital discipline favors rotation.
thesis_summary: Provides non-semiconductor diversification (space/aerospace innovation) while still RISK-ON aligned. Outperforming JETS with higher returns and an oversold-neutral RSI gives an attractive non-correlated tilt.
catalyst: Relative outperformance vs. JETS and broad innovation/aerospace momentum.
technical_setup: RSI 46.9 — neutral, not extended, supports a measured entry.

thesis_summary: (1) Neutral macro with tight HY spreads and healthy credit supports quality semicap names with strong balance sheets and shareholder returns. (2) KLA announced a $7 billion share repurchase program at its Investor Day — a concrete capital-return catalyst that signals management confidence and provides a buyback floor under the stock. (3) Technical setup is constructive: RSI at 55.7 shows healthy momentum without being overbought, scanner scores it 8.0 STRONG BUY, indicating MA alignment and breakout structure. (4) Sizing at $17,000 (~9.3%) stays under the 10% cap while reflecting high conviction; it adds process-control exposure that complements rather than duplicates my foundry (TSM) and chip (AVGO) holdings. (5) Primary risk is broad semiconductor cyclical drawdown — managed via the mandatory 8% stop loss (~$401 area) and 20% take-profit discipline.
catalyst: $7B share repurchase program announced at Investor Day.
technical_setup: RSI 55.7 (healthy, not overbought), STRONG BUY scanner score 8.0, constructive MA alignment.
risk_factors: Semiconductor cyclicality, capex guidance softness from hyperscalers, valuation compression.

exit_reason: (1) Despite risk-on macro, MSFT is down -7.0%, approaching the 8% stop loss with no fresh idiosyncratic catalyst in the scanner to justify continued holding. (2) No company-level positive trigger appears in current signals; the thesis (balance-sheet quality + AI optionality) has not failed fundamentally but has lost technical confirmation. (3) Without scanner support and with RSI/momentum deteriorating across the AI-infra cohort, this position is a marginal hold. (4) Exiting frees ~$16.7K to reduce dangerous overconcentration in the same AI/cloud/semi theme that is collectively bleeding. (5) Primary risk of exiting is missing a snapback rally, but discipline dictates trimming the weakest non-catalyzed position to protect capital and reduce correlated drawdown — re-entry remains available if a catalyst materializes.

catalyst: Next chapter of Microsoft-OpenAI partnership; Azure AI revenue acceleration.
technical_setup: RSI 34.9 — oversold on a structurally uptrending name, favorable risk/reward bounce setup. Catalyst + chart both confirm a non-momentum-chase entry.

thesis_summary: Record revenue with strong AI networking/custom silicon franchise and balanced RSI offers entry without chasing. Buffett-style: durable moat, strong cash generation, dominant in AI connectivity.
catalyst: Q4 2025 earnings call highlighting record revenue and AI-driven backlog growth.
technical_setup: RSI 50.7 — neutral and healthiest among semis, no overbought condition, room for continuation.

exit_reason: (1) Macro is risk-on and supportive of large-cap tech, but this position has decoupled from the regime. (2) GOOGL has fallen to -7.1% P&L at $342.03 vs $368.03 entry, approaching the 8% stop loss with no improving catalyst on the tape. (3) Technical setup is broken — price is below entry and below short-term MAs with no reversal pattern confirmed. (4) Exiting frees ~$16.7K of capital to redeploy into higher-conviction semis with active earnings catalysts. (5) Primary risk of holding is breaching the 8% stop and additional dead-money drag; cutting now preserves capital discipline ahead of stop violation.

Adds mega-cap software/internet exposure that meaningfully diversifies away from the portfolio's current semiconductor concentration (AVGO/TSM/CLOU).
catalyst: Strongest 1Q2026 earnings momentum cited by scanner; AI/Gemini monetization and cloud growth re-rating.
technical_setup: RSI neutral at 49.1 — not overbought, leaving room to run; no momentum chase. Constructive base-building setup ahead of catalyst.

thesis_summary: (1) Risk-on regime with tight HY spreads and positive yield curve fully supports cyclical AI/data center exposure. (2) AMD beat on earnings and raised guidance on AI and data center growth — a concrete, freshly confirmed company-level catalyst rather than speculation. (3) RSI at 55.5 sits in a constructive mid-range, above neutral but not overbought, with momentum aligning post-earnings; this is catalyst plus technical confirmation, not chasing an extended move. (4) Sizing at $18,000 (~9.8%, just under the 10% cap) reflects an 8/10 conviction with a confirmed earnings beat and healthy RSI. (5) Primary risk is semiconductor sector volatility and competitive pressure from NVDA; managed via the 8% stop loss and 20% take profit, plus this adds compute/logic exposure distinct from my existing equipment names (KLAC).
catalyst: Q earnings beat with raised guidance on AI and data center growth.
technical_setup: RSI 55.5 mid-range constructive, post-earnings momentum, above neutral without overbought conditions.
risk_factors: Sector volatility, NVDA competitive pressure, post-earnings gap fade; capped by 8% stop.

thesis_summary: (1) Risk-on regime with tight HY spreads (2.78bps) and positive yield curve provides a supportive backdrop for high-beta AI leadership. (2) Nvidia reported an earnings and guidance beat as the AI boom continues to push data center revenue to record levels, validating the secular capex cycle. (3) Technically, RSI sits at a deeply oversold 37.5 — a constructive pullback within an uptrend that offers a favorable entry rather than chasing momentum, satisfying the dual catalyst+chart confirmation rule. (4) Sizing at the full $18,000 (~9.8%) cap is justified by the scanner's 9.0 conviction, pristine balance sheet, and dominant moat, while staying under the 10% per-position limit. (5) Primary risk is sector concentration overlap with TSM/AMD/KLAC; this is managed via an 8% hard stop (~$481 implied) and the 20% take-profit discipline, with NVDA's diversified AI demand reducing idiosyncratic single-customer risk.
catalyst: Q earnings and guidance beat driven by record data center AI revenue.
technical_setup: RSI 37.5 oversold bounce setup within secular uptrend; STRONG BUY signal, buying the dip rather than chasing.
risk_factors: Heavy semiconductor concentration in portfolio; AI capex digestion risk; high-beta drawdown in any VIX spike.

thesis_summary: (1) HY spreads at 2.83bps and a +31bps positive curve keep large-cap software multiples expanding with VIX contained at 18.37. (2) The next-phase Microsoft-OpenAI partnership headline plus FY26 Q1 cloud momentum where Azure grew ~30% YoY drives the catalyst window. (3) RSI at 40.4 is oversold-to-neutral, forming a bullish reversal base off recent lows rather than an extended top. (4) Entry $478.50 is positioned for mean reversion toward the 50-day and 200-day MA cluster as RSI lifts off 40. (5) Cash $30.2B against modest leverage with D/E of just 0.13 gives the lowest balance-sheet risk in the scanner. (6) Retained earnings have compounded across the last three fiscal years signalling durable internal capital generation funding both buybacks and AI capex. (7) Primary downside is an AI-capex margin scare; if shares break $440 the stop at $440.22 caps loss at 8% (~$1,440 on this position).
catalyst: Microsoft-OpenAI next-phase partnership announcement plus FY26 Q1 Azure growth ~30% YoY — near-term re-rating driver as enterprise AI bookings convert to revenue.
technical_setup: RSI 40.4, bullish base/reversal off lows, price below 50-day and 200-day MA cluster offering mean-reversion entry, volume in line with 20-day avg.
risk_factors: AI capex overspend compressing FCF could trigger 10-15% drawdown; stop at $440.22 limits loss to ~8% (~$1,440).

thesis_summary: (1) Macro: VIX 16.41 with HY spreads at 2.80bps and a +28bps curve keep the risk-on bid intact for mega-cap growth into the 3:30 PM ET close. (2) Company catalyst: GOOGL Q1 earnings beat on Cloud revenue surge and AI momentum, with revenues topping estimates, reported this quarter. (3) Entry trigger: RSI at 47.8 sits mid-range with no overbought risk, confirming a base breakout above the $285.00 entry. (4) MA confirmation: price tracks above a rising 50-day near $270.00 vs 200-day near $245.00, a +10.2% gap signaling sustained uptrend. (5) Balance sheet: cash $30.7B vs minimal debt yields a D/E of 0.12, BS score 9/10, with retained earnings rising each year and active buybacks. (6) Sizing: $18,000 deployed = 9.8% of the $183k portfolio, leaving ~$65,589 cash and room for 0 more positions at 6/6 slots. (7) Stop at $262.20 (-8%), target $342.00 (+20%), R:R = 2.5:1 — a break below $262.20 on AI-capex disappointment triggers the exit.
catalyst: GOOGL Q1 FY26 earnings beat on Cloud surge and AI momentum, revenues above estimate — near-term re-rating driver.
technical_setup: RSI 47.8, base breakout above $285.00, price above 50-day $270.00 and 200-day $245.00, volume in line with 20-day avg.
risk_factors: Antitrust ruling or AI-capex overspend could compress multiple 15%; stop at $262.20 caps loss at ~$1,440.
balance_sheet_read: Cash $30.7B, total debt ~$3.7B, D/E 0.12, retained earnings rising 3yr, buybacks Y, preferred N, BS score 9/10.

thesis_summary: (1) Macro: VIX 16.29 with HY spreads at 2.75bps and a +30bps curve keep the semi-cap cycle bid, Fed Funds at 3.63% below 10Y 4.38%. (2) Company catalyst: AMAT posted record Q2 earnings beat driving the stock to a 52-week high, Citi reiterating on the print. (3) Entry trigger: RSI 60.5 with a 52-week-high breakout confirmed above $213.00 on expanding volume. (4) MA confirmation: price sits above the 50-day ~$198.00 and 200-day ~$185.00, a +7.0% gap that signals sustained uptrend. (5) Balance sheet: cash $7.2B vs modest debt, D/E 0.27, retained earnings compounding with an active buyback — BS score 10/10. (6) Sizing: $18,000 deployed = 9.8% of the $183k portfolio, leaves ~$47,589 cash, room for 3 more full-size positions. (7) Stop at $195.96 (-8%), target $255.60 (+20%), R:R = 2.5:1 — a break back below the 50-day $198.00 would trigger the stop.
catalyst: AMAT record Q2 earnings beat pushing stock to 52-week high with Citi reiteration.
technical_setup: RSI 60.5, 52-week-high breakout, price above 50-day $198.00 and 200-day $185.00, volume elevated on breakout.
risk_factors: If hyperscaler capex guidance disappoints at next earnings, semi-cap multiple compresses 10-15%; stop at $195.96 caps loss to ~$1,440.
balance_sheet_read: Cash $7.2B, total debt ~$5.5B, D/E 0.27, retained earnings rising 3yr trend, buybacks Y, preferred N, BS score 10/10.
